title: Navagraha Jain Temple
tagline: Navagraha Jain Temple — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: Navagraha Jain Temple or Navagraha Teertha or Navagraha Tirtha is situated at Varur near Hubli, Karnataka.
overview.description: Navagraha Jain Temple or Navagraha Teertha or Navagraha Tirtha is situated at Varur near Hubli, Karnataka. Navagraha Teertha is one of the major pilgrim spots for the Jain community in India. The temple features a 61-foot (19 m) tall monolithic idol of the Shri 1008 Bhagavan Parshvanatha and the smaller statues of the other eight Jain teerthankaras. The statue is the tallest statue of the Jain deity Parshvanatha in India and weighs 185 tons.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Navagraha Jain Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Navagraha Jain Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Navagraha Jain Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Hubli Airport (17 km) and the nearest railway station is Hubballi Junction (14 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Navagraha Jain Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Navagraha Jain Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Navagraha Jain Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Navagraha Jain Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Navagraha Jain Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Palikop, Kurdikeri, Kamplikop, Dyamapur, Katnur.
